name: Spoiler Hygiene
summary: Player-facing surfaces must never reveal twists, secrets, or backstory that the narrative intends to reveal through play. Keep canon separate from codex.

rules.statement: Player-facing surfaces must never reveal twists the narrative intends to reveal through play
Player-facing surfaces must never reveal backstory that should emerge through discovery
Player-facing surfaces must never expose internal mechanics or system names

counter_examples: Codeword names or gate logic (CODEWORD: …, flag=true, DC checks, RNG)
Determinism or production details (seeds, models, DAW, plugins, sampler settings)
Canon twists, hidden allegiances, behind-the-scenes causes
Internal identifiers (schema field names, section IDs as canonical truth, file paths)

definitions.meaning: Spoiler-level truth about the world. What REALLY happened and why.
distinguishing_features: Contains hidden histories and causal relationships

definitions.meaning: Player-safe reference entries. What players have learned or could learn.

examples: 'The foreman eyes your badge; his smile doesn't reach his eyes.'
'No union token on your lapel; the foreman waves you back.'

counter_examples: 'Rendered in SDXL, seed 1234; stinger at -10 LUFS.'
warnings.prevention: Technique is recorded in logs, not on surfaces.

warnings.consequence: Players learn secrets they should discover.
counter_examples: 'Dock 7 fire was sabotage by Toll Syndicate.'
examples: 'A refinery accident years ago reshaped safety drills.'

warnings.consequence: Players see interface instead of story.

counter_examples: 'Choice A is better for Act II.'
warnings.prevention: Keep choices neutral; translators preserve contrast, not outcomes.

criteria.name: Diegetic Test for Surfaces
criteria.pass_condition: Before any text reaches a player surface, answer: (1) Could a character say this? (2) Does it reveal more than the player should know? (3) Would reading this spoil a discovery?
criteria.reasoning: If it fails, move content to canon or remove it.

heuristics.context: Deciding what belongs on player surfaces
heuristics.guidance: Keep wonder on the page and machinery off it. If a player could infer a twist or see the gears, it doesn't belong on the surface.
